content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size 2N2N2N, where every number from 111 to NNN occurs **exactly twice**.

Consider choosing a subsequence of positions t=(t1,t2,…,tM)t = (t_1, t_2, \ldots, t_M)t=(t1​,t2​,…,tM​) with 1≤t1<t2<⋯<tM≤2N1 \le t_1 < t_2 < \cdots < t_M \le 2N1≤t1​<t2​<⋯<tM​≤2N.

This subsequence is called **valid** if for every starting point jjj with 1≤j≤M−N+11 \le j \le M - N + 11≤j≤M−N+1, the values

Atj,Atj+1,…,Atj+N−1A_{t_j}, A_{t_{j+1}}, \ldots, A_{t_{j+N-1}}Atj​​,Atj+1​​,…,Atj+N−1​​ are all **mutually different**.

That is, within every sliding window of length NNN inside the chosen subsequence, no value appears more than once.
Your task is to determine the **largest possible size** of such a valid subsequence.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT — the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines:

The first line contains single integer NNN, where NNN is the number of distinct values.
The second line contains 2N2N2N integers A1,A2,…,A2NA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_{2N}A1​,A2​,…,A2N​.




Output Format
For each test case, print one integer - the **maximum** possible length of a valid subsequence.
constraints: Each integer from 111 to NNN occurs exactly twice in the array.
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For Test Case 1:

Window size = 1, and single elements are always distinct. We can take both.
Hence answer is 2.

For Test Case 2:

You can pick one of each: {1,2,3}.
Any extra pick repeats a number inside a window of size 3.
Hence, answer is 3.
